name: Geography of India: Location & Physical Features

researchNotes: Strong real evidence — 4 confirmed hits across 2022/2024/2025, plus a further 10-question real past-paper set from web research. First topic in Part A Unit II (Geography of India), opening this unit the same way Scientific Temper & Reasoning opened Unit I.

reading.intro: This is the first of Unit II's sub-topics, covering India's location, landforms, plateaus, rivers, and borders. Real exam evidence favors statement-evaluation ('which of these is/are correct') and superlative facts (longest, oldest, biggest) over plain description.

qa.question: Real exam: Evaluate these statements about the Malwa Plateau — (1) average altitude 600m, slopes east (2) mostly drained by the Chambal and its tributaries (3) located in Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Madhya Pradesh.
qa.answer: (2) and (3) are correct; (1) is false (the altitude/slope-direction claim doesn't hold). Malwa Plateau is genuinely drained by the Chambal river system and spans Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Madhya Pradesh.

qa.question: Real exam: Evaluate these statements about Indian soils — (1) northern plains' soils are largely deposited by Himalayan rivers (2) iron oxide presence is more characteristic of black soil (3) Deccan-trap-derived soil is fertile with high moisture-holding capacity.
qa.answer: (1) and (3) are correct; (2) is false — high iron oxide content is actually characteristic of RED soil (which gets its colour from iron oxide), not black soil. Black soil (regur) is derived from basalt/Deccan trap, is clay-rich, and is well suited to cotton cultivation.

qa.question: Real exam: What is the northern upland of the Karnataka Plateau called?
Real exam: What is the largest west-flowing river of peninsular India?
qa.answer: The Narmada (not Mahi, Sabarmati, or Luni).

qa.question: What is the height of Anaimudi (the highest peak of the Peninsular Plateau / Western Ghats) above mean sea level?
What is the approximate length of the Himalayan range, and which mountain system in India is the OLDEST?
qa.answer: Himalayan range length ≈ 2,560 km. The Aravalli Range is the oldest Indian mountain system (older than the Himalayas, Siwaliks, or Western Ghats).

qa.question: With which neighbouring country does India share its LONGEST international border?
Which country in the world shares a border with the maximum number of other countries?
Which is India's first Island district?
qa.answer: Majuli (a river island in the Brahmaputra, Assam — notable as a district that is also a river island, distinct from the Andaman & Nicobar or Lakshadweep island UTs).

qa.question: Arrange these Himalayan mountain ranges from north to south: Karakoram, Ladakh, Pir-Panjal, Zaskar.
qa.answer: Karakoram → Ladakh → Zaskar → Pir-Panjal (north to south).

questions.prompt: The Malwa Plateau is mostly drained by which river and its tributaries?
Which colour of soil is most associated with high iron oxide content?
questions.explanation: Red soil gets its colour from iron oxide content.

questions.prompt: Soil derived from the Deccan trap is known for:
options: Poor fertility and low moisture retention
High fertility and high moisture-holding capacity
questions.explanation: Deccan trap-derived (black/regur) soil is fertile with high moisture retention.

questions.prompt: The northern upland of the Karnataka Plateau is known as:
The largest west-flowing river of peninsular India is the:
The height of Anaimudi above mean sea level is approximately:
questions.explanation: 2,695 m — the highest peak of the Peninsular Plateau.

questions.prompt: Which is the oldest mountain system in India?
India shares its longest international border with:
Which country shares a border with the maximum number of other countries?
Which is India's first Island district?
questions.explanation: Majuli, a river island district in the Brahmaputra (Assam).
